chapter: ENG9_4 – In the Kingdom of Fools
title: In the Kingdom of Fools – Easy LAQs

questions.question: Describe the strange rule introduced by the king in the Kingdom of Fools.
questions.answer: The king ordered that night should be treated as day and day as night. People had to work during the night and sleep during the day. Anyone who disobeyed the rule would be punished with death.

questions.question: How did the guru and disciple react when they first entered the kingdom?
questions.answer: They were surprised to see the whole town sleeping during the day. Later they discovered the unusual rule that people worked at night and slept during the day.

questions.question: Why did the guru decide to leave the kingdom?
questions.answer: The guru realised that the kingdom was ruled by fools. He believed that living in such a place would be dangerous, so he decided to leave immediately.

questions.question: Why did the disciple refuse to leave the kingdom?
questions.answer: The disciple stayed because all goods in the market cost the same price. He could buy plenty of food cheaply and live comfortably.

questions.question: Describe the incident that led to the court case.
questions.answer: A thief entered a merchant’s house to steal goods. While leaving, the weak wall of the house collapsed and killed him. His brother complained to the king demanding justice.

questions.question: Why was the merchant blamed for the thief’s death?
questions.answer: The king believed that the merchant was responsible because the wall of his house collapsed and killed the thief.

questions.question: Why did the bricklayer blame the dancing girl?
questions.answer: The bricklayer said that the dancing girl distracted him with the sound of her anklets while he was building the wall.

questions.question: How did the dancing girl defend herself in the court?
questions.answer: She said she was walking up and down the street because the goldsmith kept delaying the jewellery he was making for her.

questions.question: Why was the goldsmith blamed in the case?
questions.answer: The goldsmith admitted he delayed the dancer because he was busy completing the merchant’s order.

questions.question: Why could the merchant not be executed?
questions.answer: The merchant was too thin to fit the execution stake, so the king’s men searched for another person who was fat enough.

questions.question: Why was the disciple chosen for execution?
questions.answer: The disciple had become very fat because he ate cheap food every day. Therefore he was chosen to fit the stake.

questions.question: How did the disciple react when he was taken for execution?
questions.answer: He was frightened and remembered the guru’s warning that the kingdom was dangerous.

questions.question: How did the guru come to know about the disciple’s danger?
questions.answer: The guru had magical powers and saw the situation in a vision. Therefore he returned to save the disciple.

questions.question: What trick did the guru play to save the disciple?
questions.answer: He told the king that whoever died first on the stake would be reborn as the king of the kingdom.

questions.question: Why did the king want to die first?
questions.answer: The king believed that dying first on the stake would make him king again in the next life.

questions.question: What decision did the king and minister finally take?
questions.answer: They secretly replaced the guru and disciple and went to the execution stake themselves.

questions.question: What happened after the execution?
questions.answer: The people discovered that the king and minister were dead and the kingdom was left without rulers.

questions.question: Why did the people ask the guru to become their king?
questions.answer: The people realised that they needed a wise and intelligent ruler to govern the kingdom.

questions.question: Who became the minister of the kingdom?
questions.answer: The disciple became the minister of the kingdom.

questions.question: What condition did the guru place before ruling the kingdom?
questions.answer: He said he would rule only if he could change the foolish laws of the kingdom.

questions.question: What reforms did the guru introduce?
questions.answer: He restored the normal system where people worked during the day and slept at night.

questions.question: Why is the story humorous?
questions.answer: The foolish actions of the king and minister create many funny and absurd situations.

questions.question: What lesson does the story teach?
questions.answer: The story teaches that wisdom and intelligence are more important than foolish authority.

questions.question: Why is the story considered a folktale?
questions.answer: It is considered a folktale because it uses simple storytelling and humour to teach a moral lesson.

questions.question: Explain the central idea of 'In the Kingdom of Fools'.
questions.answer: The central idea of the story is that foolish leadership creates chaos, while wisdom restores order and justice.
